title: Cryptic error messages when trying to use packed and unpacked array parameters
body:
On this issue I'm reporting:
* A bug(?) I found when trying to declare a parameter as a *packed* array (note that issue #846 refers to *unpacked* arrays).
* A suggestion/request to improve the error message displayed on unsupported features.
________

I was trying to simulate this code (well, not this code exactly but a similar one), which seems to use an unsupported feature (unpacked array parameters):
```sv
module TEST_param_arr;

parameter int num = 6;
parameter int vars [0:num-1] = '{4, 8, 15, 16, 23, 42};

initial begin
    for (int i=0; i<num; i++)
        $display("vars[%0d] = %2d", i, vars[i]);
    $finish;
end

endmodule
```
and got this rather cryptic error message:
```
TEST_param_arr.sv:4: error: parameter declared outside parameter port list must have a default value.
TEST_param_arr.sv:4: syntax error
TEST_param_arr.sv:4: error: invalid module item.
```
I managed to figure out that this was due to issue #846 *(Parameters having an **unpacked** array type is a SystemVerilog feature that isn't supported yet)*, but iverilog could at least try to figure out what I was trying to do and tell me that that's not supported; the error message as it is is quite confusing.

Now, since a comment in that issue mentions that I cannot use *unpacked* arrays, I thought OK, I'll use a *packed* array instead:
```sv
module TEST_param_arr2;

parameter int num = 6;
parameter [0:num-1] [31:0] vars = {32'd4, 32'd8, 32'd15, 32'd16, 32'd23, 32'd42};

initial begin
    for (int i=0; i<num; i++)
        $display("vars[%0d] = %2d", i, vars[i]);
    $finish;
end

endmodule
```
and got this much more colorful error message:
```
TEST_param_arr2.sv:8: assert: ../iverilog/netmisc.cc:1821: failed assertion packed_dims.size() == 1
```
I honestly don't know how to interpret that error message, other than it seeming to be related to the use of packed dimensions.
In this case, the issue seems to be in the *use* of the variable though; for some reason using a single index on a multidimensional packed parameter.  I tried replacing `vars[i]` with `vars[i][31:0]` with no luck.

Apparently the only way around this issue is to declare the parameter as `parameter [0:32*num-1] vars = {32'd4, 32'd8, 32'd15, 32'd16, 32'd23, 32'd42};` and then use `vars[32*i+:32]` in the code, which isn't very readable or maintainable.
________
OS: Windows
Icarus Verilog version 12.0 (devel) (s20150603-1539-g2693dd32b)
code_blocks:
TEST_param_arr.sv:4: error: parameter declared outside parameter port list must have a default value.
TEST_param_arr.sv:4: syntax error
TEST_param_arr.sv:4: error: invalid module item.
